body_txt: As opposed to the comfort of blue with white or the malice of black with red , blue and black cultivate an aura of obscurity and dread. A combination so empty and lifeless, the colors come across as intimidating and unfamiliar despite how subdued they seem together. Humans hold this inherent terror because the subconscious fears the unknown ; life, light, and warmth are so integral to one's security that any image even remotely resembling a dark space is unsettling to imagine. These colors, although visually distinct, are so conceptually close together that they create a palette as close to darkness as one can get while still having color involved. Places that come to mind include low-lit settings like a Creepy Cemetery , an Eldritch Ocean Abyss , or a dark forest when things appear less as they seem, but a sense of death is present in all of them. For those who wander alone into a pitch-black ambience with blue shades that only seem to highlight how bleak it is, one can't help but sense the sinister side of these seemingly mundane places, and it will leave them with a sense of hopelessness and panic toward whatever lurks in the shadows. Thus, the combo not only represents fear — it embodies complete human detachment. The use of black with blue is generally the main component of genres such as noir , Horror , and Sci-Fi ; the aesthetic of Goth characters; the uniforms of the police; the uncharted territory of advanced technology; the cold dead of winter ; or a starless night sky , when cool colors are prevalent. Often, this trope can be used for Color-Coded Elements in both An Ice Person and blue fire (especially if it's Hellfire ) if black is added to the intensity. The trope may be averted if used as a shortcut into Darker and Edgier , though subversions and downplays can occasionally hint at its grim undertone. As black and blue are the primary colors of nighttime , darkness or shadow -themed entities may feature them with an optional dash of Lunacy , as well as for supernatural works that have a God of the Dead or a Monochrome Apparition . When light is involved, it creates a harsh case of Unnaturally Blue Lighting . This trope is used in both Dark Is Evil and Dark Is Not Evil , as well as Graying Morality , with the former often for subtle villains who don't oversell their villainy, but are still cruel and threatening sociopaths, and the latter often the choice for a no-nonsense Terror Hero . While black is almost always the same (unless it's dark gray), blue, as the lighter part, can range from light-cyan to cobalt-navy. Associations of a blue hue will also carry different connotations: light or "icy" blue juxtaposes against black much more intensely, while cobalt blue melds with it. Thus, icy blue will be used for ice , supernatural , or insanity , or in some cases Sci-Fi or Cyberpunk settings, while cobalt blue will indicate night , deep water , or depression . Aside from blue, other adjacent colors of the cool color spectrum, such as green and purple, can be involved with the color scheme as teal and indigo hues, in reference to the real-life black iridescence that features these colors. If the blue (or purple) is so dark that it blurs into black and/or becomes one color that's referred to exclusively as "black", it falls under a different trope: Purple Is the New Black . A Sub-Trope of Good Colors, Evil Colors . A Sister Trope to Evil Wears Black . Opposite Trope and Color Contrast to White and Red and Eerie All Over and Gold and White Are Divine . Can be combined with Red and Black and Evil All Over or Black and Yellow and Alarming All Over if red or yellow, respectively, are added to the mix. Compare to Purple Is Impure , which embodies purple as a corruptive color, and Paranormal Palette for another creepy color combination . Compare Creepy Blue Eyes (or Icy Blue Eyes ), which are often used with Black Eyes of Crazy , and Evil Is Deathly Cold , which tends to be portrayed in front of a black background. Also compare Grayscale of Evil and Undeathly Pallor when The Undead have this scheme due to being deceased. Contrast Heavenly Blue , Blue Is Heroic , and Primary-Color Champion . See also Blue Means Cold . Not to be confused with the euphemism black-and-blue. Or this dress ◊ for that matter.
